title: Correct promoted-path safety and usability
action: Implement D8 as a bounded correction to the promoted first-run path. Bind demo mode to 127.0.0.1 by default while preserving an explicit bind override. In BOTH the generated and packaged production units, remove ProtectHome=read-only (or set ProtectHome=false) while retaining NoNewPrivileges=true and ProtectSystem=strict; ReadWritePaths alone cannot restore user-workspace writes under ProtectHome. Immediately after OS/architecture checks and before the first migrate_dir call, require command -v systemctl and the side-effect-free probe systemctl --user show-environment >/dev/null 2>&1. No mv, mkdir, binary/unit/env/config write, rm, or service command may precede this preflight. Narrow docs to systemd-based Linux with a reachable per-user manager. State that only a newly generated token is printed. For repeat/migrated installs, document retrieval from ${XDG_CONFIG_HOME:-$HOME/.config}/herdr-go/herdctl.env and rotation by rewriting HERDCTL_WEB_SECRET with file mode 600 then restarting the user service; never place the token in a journal/log command. Extend the contract test with directive-preservation and source-order assertions.

truths: Demo mode listens only on loopback by default despite using the memorable demo credential
A user can explicitly override the demo bind when they intentionally want network exposure
Both generated and packaged units omit ProtectHome=read-only (or set ProtectHome=false) while retaining NoNewPrivileges=true and ProtectSystem=strict
The installer proves systemctl exists and systemctl --user show-environment succeeds immediately after platform checks and before the first migration or mutation
No move, directory creation, installation, removal, file write, or service command occurs before the systemd user-manager preflight
Docs name systemd-based Linux with a reachable per-user manager as the one-command boundary
Only a newly generated token is printed; repeat/migrated retrieval and safe rotation use the canonical env file without logging the secret

artifacts.substantive: loopback demo default with explicit override preserved
exact pre-mutation systemctl/show-environment preflight and generated service policy compatible with writable user workspaces
NoNewPrivileges and ProtectSystem retained without blanket ProtectHome read-only behavior
source-order assertions for preflight before migration/mutation; both unit forms omit ProtectHome read-only and retain non-home hardening; docs/runtime contract checks
safe demo and honest systemd-user-manager install/login guidance
first-install token behavior plus canonical env-file retrieval and mode-600 rotation/restart steps

key_links: README demo command matches the runtime loopback default
install.sh generated unit and packaging/herdr-go.service both retain NoNewPrivileges=true and ProtectSystem=strict while allowing user-home workspace writes
command -v systemctl and systemctl --user show-environment occur before the first migrate_dir and every mutating command
installation/usage token recovery matches ${XDG_CONFIG_HOME:-$HOME/.config}/herdr-go/herdctl.env created or migrated by install.sh
token rotation preserves env-file mode 600 and restarts herdr-go without echoing the token to logs
prohibitions: Do not use the known demo credential on a non-loopback default bind
Do not remove NoNewPrivileges=true or ProtectSystem=strict
Do not rely on ReadWritePaths to override ProtectHome=read-only
Do not use systemctl --user is-system-running as the reachability probe
Do not mutate existing user state before prerequisite checks pass
Do not print an existing login token during upgrade or include it in journal/log commands
Do not claim generic Linux support beyond a reachable systemd user manager
Do not touch files outside this cell

verify: cargo fmt --all --check && cargo test --quiet --lib && cargo clippy --quiet -- -D warnings && bash -n install.sh && bash tests/rename_contract.sh && cd web && npm run bundle && npm run test -- --run

trace.outcome: Made repeat and migrated token recovery XDG-aware with explicit safe rotation steps, and strengthened the rename contract across both unit forms and every preflight-protected mutation class.
